name: Ranitidine
executiveSummary: H2 receptor antagonist, antiulcer agent, stress ulcer prevention.
indications:
## Main indications

H2 receptor antagonist, antiulcer agent, stress ulcer prevention.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.dose: Preterm and term infants < 2 weeks: IV: loading dose: 1.5 mg/kg, maintenance: 1.5 mg/kg/day every 12 h. Children: IV: 2-4 mg/kg/day every 6-8 h. Adults: IV: 50 mg/dose every 6-8 h, maximum dose: 400 mg/day.
pediatrico.notes: For IV administration dilute in 5% dextrose or SWFI to a concentration of 0.5 mg/mL (maximum: 2.5 mg/mL) and infuse over 15-30 minutes. Caution in patients with hepatic insufficiency.

stability:
## Pediatric guide

- 48 h at room temperature.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Dizziness, insomnia, agitation, skin rash, constipation, nausea, diarrhea, vomiting, headache. Bradycardia with rapid infusion. Rare: increased transaminases, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, bronchospasm.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — Ranitidine Hydrochloride (code 0175, ATC A02BA).
